## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about several ways to use webinars to test whether a customer is interested in buying a product or not. So let me give you several examples. The first one is, let's imagine that you have a multi-product business. So let's just use a scenario here. You've got a series of training products. You've got five different training products that you can sell to somebody. You're doing lots of different marketing. So let's say one of those is that you have an opt-in incentive. When someone signs up for your list, they get that special report and that special report is designed to sell them product number one in your portfolio products. So once they sign up for them, you then run them through the promotional sequence for that product. The entire model for that, giving them the opportunity to buy. If they do buy, then you deliver that product and then you next give them a nurture sequence to rebuild their trust so that they feel like you're not always trying to pitch them something or if they don't buy, after you get done running them through that promotional sequence, you then do the same thing. You give them again another nurture sequence to build trust. Then here's the thing that so many people don't do, which is you automate the process. So after you try to sell them product one and then whether they buy or not, you end up running them through a nurture sequence, then you automate the process to run them through a promotional sequence for another product. Basically, you keep repeating this process until you run out of products. Basically, the theory here is you build this automated system that goes through all of your products before you expose them to promoting someone else's product. So if you do this right, let's say you've got five products and each one of those sequences takes three weeks. Then you've got one week of nurture sequence before you give them the next one. You got five products. After someone comes into your world as a new lead, you could go five months of promoting one product and then the next product and then the next product. So you go through that entire process before you pitch them anyone else's product. Now, ideally, you automate that entire sequence and so it just happens. So whenever I show this model, a combination of two things happens. First off, they feel a little overwhelmed and I totally understand that. Don't worry about it. It's not all that overwhelming because you're taking promotional sequences, you've already got created,

## [03:03 - 06:05]

and you simply string them from one to the next. But there is a key question that everyone always asks and they should because it's a really good question. And that is, when you're doing this, how do I know which product to promote to them next? Well, you could just guess. Or you could run a webinar as a testing tool. Testing whether or not they're interested in that particular subject area. Now, bonus tip, don't try to sell anything on that webinar. This is basically a nurturing webinar where its purpose is to build the relationship, build trust, build your expertise in that area in their minds so that they will potentially be willing to buy later on. So the purpose of this webinar is to see if they're interested before you try to sell them anything. You're going to use probably another webinar to sell the product itself. But they're more likely to come to that because they've seen you do another webinar on that subject where you didn't try to sell anything. And so now you're adding value again can actually increase your attendance at that selling webinar because you ran a non-selling webinar before you tried to sell them something. So this is a really simple sequence. Two to three emails establishing the problem and giving them some tactical solutions. And then just like we talked about before, this is basic webinar funnel formula. Then we announce, and I can see from the comments that this is an important subject. And so I'm going to do a webinar to help solve this problem. And then you announce this webinar. And again, this webinar is not a sales webinar. You don't tell them that. You just announce the webinar. So then people who sign up get tagged with an interest tag. So in my email program, I have a set of tags that are called interested space dash space, whatever topic that is. I also have a set of tags that say not interested space dash space, whatever that topic area is. So I'm able to very quickly see who's interested in that particular topic area. Later on, whether I'm promoting my own products or promoting someone else's products. I know I've got a group of people who have at least at some point in the past expressed interest in that particular subject area. So that's the group of people who will sign up for that. But what about that pesky 70% we keep talking about who don't sign up for webinars. Well, I have two determiners that I use to determine to decide whether I'm going to give them the interest to tag or the not interested tag. So the first question is, did they click on any of the emails in the sequence? The second one is I asked them.

## [06:06 - 09:07]

Basically, I asked them, are you interested in knowing more about solving x problem. So I, so here's what I've done. I sent them the two to three emails. I've announced the webinar, I promoted the webinar, they've not signed up for the webinar. Then I come back to them with an email that says, Hey, are you interested in knowing more about solving this problem? If they say yes, they get the interest to tag. If they say no, they get the not interested tag and I tag them appropriately. Now, if they don't answer that question, because their answer is the key, that answer determines what which one of those two tags they get. If they don't answer, then I look at the clicks. And what I usually look at is in this entire sequence where I talked about this particular subject area, did they click on at least two of the links that I had in there? If they're not clicking and they didn't answer, that's a pretty good indication that they're not interested. So I give them the not interested tag and I move them on to the next project in the sequence. So what we're trying to do here is to quickly determine potential interest without wasting time or burning your your list. So they came in because they were interested in X product and now you're trying to sell them Y product. So that's not what they came in and saying they were interested in. So the last thing I want to do is spend a whole month talking about Y product when they're not interested in that. And so I'm trying to come up with a way and a webinar is a great way to do that to determine whether that interest is there or not. So if they're interested, they get the full promotional sequence. If they're not interested, I move them on to the next product sequence. So all this is based on a webinar that doesn't sell them anything. Bonus tip. I suggest you regularly run webinars that don't sell anything. What? I thought this was all about selling stuff using webinars. Well, here's what happens when you regularly run webinars that don't sell anything, it trains your audiences that they want to attend your webinars. It gives back to them and builds trust and allows you to test their interest because this webinar could be a non-selling webinar in their mind or it could be a selling webinar. They don't know in advance because bonus tip, you're resisting the temptation to say, hey, nothing for sale here and your promotion for the non-selling webinars. You want it to be totally opaque as a non-transparent, whether you're going to sell them something on this webinar or not, because we want to train them to come to webinars, not to train them to come to the ones that say that you're not going to sell anything. Another bonus tip. Consider adding surveys into this process. You can add surveys during the webinar or after the webinar containing questions that specifically test their purchase interest. Would you be interested in a product on the subject?

## [09:09 - 10:10]

So have you got a new product idea? Let's ignore whether or not you've got this whole multi-product business or not. Let's just look at this whole concept of using webinars as a testing mechanism. So let's just imagine you wake up one morning and during the middle of the night, you came up with this amazing new idea. All right, great. Try running a non-selling webinar on the subject to see if people are interested. So with the days worth of work, writing that webinar, writing the promotions for it, you can basically see whether you've got enough interest to make it worth your while. And I strongly recommend doing that over, hey, I'm going to spend three months and create a whole product and I'm going to spend another month creating all the marketing and everything else like that, only to find out that no one's interested in that product in the first place. This is a great way to use a webinar to determine whether people are interested in this product or not.
